title: What is the largest amount you've held?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for examining your history with big balances.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for examining your track record with holding money, what happened next when large sums arrived, and what a steadier relationship with a full account might look like.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when a large amount has come in and you notice yourself watching the number rather than trusting it.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. The fear of a full account often has a history: a specific time a big sum disappeared, a family rule about not having too much. These prompts look for that history.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What emotions come up when I imagine a large balance sitting in my account?
Write without editing for two minutes. Name the emotion as precisely as you can.
What part of me feels uneasy about holding a large sum?
Write what the uneasy part says. Where did it learn this?
What is the largest amount of money I've ever held? What happened next?
Write the specific number if you remember it. Write what you did: spent it, gave it away, watched it drain, or kept it.
What have I already proven I can hold onto successfully?
Money, commitments, a home, a relationship, a habit. Write the specific thing. You have evidence.
What would it look like to hold a large sum and feel nothing but ordinary about it?
Write the scene: the account open, the number there, your response. What are you doing instead of watching it?
Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
